summary: Decompiled nine fighter-related functions across Crazy Hand, Popo/Ice Climbers specials, and common fighter camera logic. The PR replaced placeholder markers with matching C implementations, added small type/header refinements needed by the new code, and reported successful ninja, ninja diff, and 100% objdiff matches.
what_changed: Implemented Crazy Hand animation/transition helpers ftCh_Slam_Anim, ftCh_TagRockPaper_Anim, and ftCh_GrabUnk1_8015B670 in ftCh_Init.c.
Added extern float references in ftCh_Init.c for ftCh_Init_804DA180, ftCh_Init_804DA184, ftCh_Init_804DA1D8, and ftCh_Init_804DA1DC, used as motion-state animation/start/rate arguments.
Added an include for ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_Attack100.h in ftCh_Init.c.
Implemented Popo/Ice Climbers special functions ftPp_SpecialHiStart_0_IASA, ftPp_SpecialAirHiStart_0_IASA, ftPp_SpecialLw_Enter, ftPp_SpecialLw_Anim, and ftPp_SpecialAirLw_Anim.
Implemented ftCo_Rebirth_Cam in ft_0D31.c, updating the fighter camera box from common motion variables, ftCamera_80076018 output, and ftLib_800866DC.
Refined ftIceClimberAttributes layout by splitting padding at 0x7C and naming float x80, which is used as the left-stick threshold in SpecialHiStart IASA.
Extended ftPp_MotionVars with a speciallw struct containing x0 and bitfield x4_b0 for SpecialLw state.
Changed ftPp_SpecialS.h from a placeholder comment for fn_80122D2C to a real prototype with address comment /* 122D2C */.
smart_moves: Used identical implementations for ground and air SpecialHiStart IASA where behavior matched: check cmd_vars[0], clear it, compare ABS(lstick.x) against Ice Climber attribute x80, update facing, then rotate part Y by M_PI_2 * facing_dir.
Preserved matching-sensitive stack behavior with PAD_STACK(16) and PAD_STACK(8) in Popo special functions.

Modeled SpecialLw cleanup carefully: destroy effects when fv.pp.x2230_b0 is set, clear the flag and callbacks, reset part X rotation, then branch to ground wait-like logic via ft_8008A2BC or aerial fall via ftCo_Fall_Enter.
Introduced just enough struct knowledge to express the matched functions naturally: ftIceClimberAttributes.x80 and ftPp_MotionVars.speciallw.x4_b0.
Kept callback assignments explicit, including accessory4_cb = fn_80122D2C for SpecialLw and accessory1_cb = ftCo_800DB464 for Crazy Hand capture behavior.
For ftCo_Rebirth_Cam, reused existing camera/data helpers rather than open-coding all camera math: ftCamera_80076018 and ftLib_800866DC.
decomp_lessons: When decompiling fighter action code, command variables and motion vars often need small struct/union refinements before the function can be expressed cleanly.
Ground and aerial variants may intentionally match with duplicated bodies except for their final state transition; this PR shows that sharing assumptions should be evidence-based and not forced.
Bitfields in motion vars can be necessary for matching fighter state flags, as shown by ftPp_MotionVars.speciallw.x4_b0 at fp+2344:0.
Use named fighter attribute offsets as soon as a function proves their meaning or role; here x80 is used as a stick threshold for facing updates.
Animation-end handlers commonly guard on !ftAnim_IsFramesRemaining and then perform callback cleanup, state transitions, or velocity resets.
Boss/Crazy Hand functions may use global float constants as motion-state parameters; declaring externs for those constants can be the minimal matching step before naming them more semantically.
naming_conventions: Kept project placeholder-style names for unresolved symbols such as fn_80122D2C and ftCh_Init_804DA180 rather than inventing semantic names.
Used address-bearing function naming/comment convention in headers, changing fn_80122D2C to /* 122D2C */ void fn_80122D2C(Fighter_GObj* gobj);
New Popo motion-vars member was named by action state, speciallw, matching existing fighter move-variable organization.
Unresolved Ice Climbers fields retained offset-style names such as x80 and x4_b0, which is consistent with partial struct knowledge in the codebase.
Crazy Hand functions retained existing ftCh_* and ftCo_CaptureCrazyHand_* naming patterns.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK macros in several Popo functions to preserve stack layout for matching.
Used explicit float literals 0.0f and 1.0f in Fighter_ChangeMotionState calls where needed.
Used chained assignment for Crazy Hand self velocity reset: fp->self_vel.x = fp->self_vel.y = fp->self_vel.z = ftCh_Init_804DA178.
Used a cast/deref expression in ftCo_Rebirth_Cam, camera_box->x10.y = *(f32*) &fp->mv.co.common.x8 + spC.x0.x, indicating a matching-sensitive interpretation of a motion variable field.
Used M_PI_2 multiplied by facing_dir and cast to float before ftPartSetRotY, matching the rotation calculation shape.
Kept direct gobj->user_data access in Popo functions alongside GET_FIGHTER usage elsewhere, reflecting local matching requirements.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR body states the test plan passed: ninja builds successfully, ninja diff shows no regressions, and all functions are 100% matches in objdiff.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename ftIceClimberAttributes.x80 or ftPp_MotionVars.speciallw fields.
Search for additional decompilations of ftPp_SpecialAirLw_Enter and ftPp_SpecialLw_Coll near fn_80122D2C.
Look for future cleanup of Crazy Hand globals ftCh_Init_804DA180, ftCh_Init_804DA184, ftCh_Init_804DA1D8, and ftCh_Init_804DA1DC.
Compare ftCo_Rebirth_Cam with other fighter camera-box setup functions for common struct naming opportunities.
Search for PAD_STACK usage in Popo/Ice Climbers special move decompilations to reuse stack-matching patterns.
